system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Dayah LitWorks — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://dayah.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks
- **Docker service:** dayah-litworks_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`dayah.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → dayah-litworks_web:3000

## Pages
13 pages: Home, Servicios, Premades, Portafolio, Blog (5 posts), Nosotros, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Términos

## Design System
Dark theme (navy/plum background, #0a0a14/var(--background)). Primary: #94abd6 (muted blue). Secondary: #d43d5e (rose). Fonts: Playfair Display (headings), Inter (body). Voice: warm, creative, Spanish.

## Content
Content lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales when applicable).
All text, services, products, FAQ items are editable there.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t dayah-litworks:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Critical Patterns
- All content in content/es.json and content/en.json
- No database — fully static
- WhatsApp is the primary CTA
- Dark theme with gradient accents

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

  { key: 'Permissions-Policy', value: 'camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()' },
]

const config: NextConfig = {
  output: "standalone",
  poweredByHeader: false,
  async headers() {
    return [
      { source: '/(.*)', headers: securityHeaders },
      { source: '/images/(.*)', headers: [{ key: 'Cache-Control', value: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }] },
    ]
  },
  async redirects() {
    return [

      { source: "/ofertas", destination: "/promociones", permanent: true },

}

{"content": "FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 PORT=3000 HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
", "path": "/tmp/nudo-site/Dockerfile"}

}

beauty wellness website - generate website from leads - deploy multiple sites - paragu-ai-leads - paralell site deployment --- # Standalone E-commerce Store Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 e-commerce stores. ## Architecture Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) -> VPS -> Traefik -> service_web:3000 ## Tech Stack Next.js 15 (standalone output), React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Docker + Swarm, Traefik, SQLite (optional) ## Build & Deploy ``` npm run build docker build -t :prod . docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml ``` ## Page Structure Home, Tienda, Producto/[slug], Envio, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Terminos, Nosotros, Promociones, Blog (posts + categories + [slug]), Sitemap, Error, Not-found, RSS ## Product Data Structure (Retail) Each product should carry rich spec data, not just name+price: ```json { "slug": "titanium", "name": "Titanium", "line": "Premium", "category": "Resorte", "price": "Gs. 1.800.000", "priceFrom": "2 plazas 140x190", "image": "/images/products/titanium.png", "description": "Premium con Euro Pillow Top.", "sizes": ["2 plazas 140x190: Gs. 1.800.000", "Queen 160x200: Gs. 2.200.000"], "firmness": "Firme", "warranty": "5 años", "weight": "Hasta 120 kg por lado", "colors": ["Gris", "Azul"], "badges": ["Pillow Top", "Antiácaros", "Más vendido"] } ``` Product pages render a specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), a measure/price table, and badges. ## Retail-Specific Features - Age-gated content (localStorage with 7-day timestamp expiry) - Discreet shipping guarantee (for adult stores) ## Content Structure content/es.json sections: siteConfig, navigation, home, products (14+ with id/slug/name/price/level/sound/safety fields), categories, faq, blog, footer, shipping, payments, socialProof, brand --- ## Comprehensive Site Audit Checklist Run on EVERY page. Be brutal. ### Audit Dimensions: 1. Visual/Design -- spacing, alignment, color, images, fonts, hierarchy, consistency 2. UX/Usability -- navigation, button placement, mobile, loading states, forms, click targets 3. Content -- copy quality, tone, grammar, completeness, placeholders, length 4. E-commerce -- conversion flow, trust signals, pricing, urgency, social proof, stock 5. Technical -- load speed, console errors, broken links, SEO, titles, structured data, a11y 6. Mobile -- viewport, tap targets, readability, nav overlap 7. Industry-specific -- privacy signals, discretion tone, age gate, comfort for first-timers 8. Fix immediately. - HIGH -- Trust-killing issues (no contact info, no product images, duplicate nav). Fix this batch. - MEDIUM -- UX friction, redundant elements. Fix alongside HIGH. - LOW -- Polish, accessibility extras. Fix when time permits. ### Batch Fix Strategy: 1. Fix all CRITICAL first (1-3 line fixes) 2. Fix all HIGH second (users notice these) 3. Fix MEDIUM alongside HIGH 4. Fix LOW last 5. Run it individually per site (not in a loop) to avoid SSH timeout on long builds: ```sql CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions ( token TEXT PRIMARY KEY, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, expires_at TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ); ``` ### Auth context patterns 1. **Always wrap children in AuthContext.Provider** — never conditionally render a fallback during loading/SSR. Children will try to call `useAuth()` and crash. Set all initial state to empty/null values. 2. **Guard localStorage** — check `typeof window !== "undefined"` before any `localStorage.getItem()` call. SSR runs this code. 3. **API helper with bearer token** — define a helper function that reads token from localStorage and attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header. This avoids repeating the pattern in every method. 4. **Auth routes pattern** — single POST /api/auth route with `action` field dispatching to login/register/me/logout branches. Rate limit with in-memory map keyed on `email:ip`. 5. **Addresses/Orders as separate routes** — PUT/DELETE on /api/addresses for CRUD, GET/POST on /api/orders. This causes `npm install` in the deps stage to skip devDependencies including `typescript`, which Next.js 15 needs to parse `next.config.ts`. Fix: Set `ENV NODE_ENV=development` in the deps stage: ```dockerfile # MANDATORY: next.config.ts must have output: "standalone" # Without this, .next/standalone/server.js won't exist and container crashes on start FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps WORKDIR /app ENV NODE_ENV=development COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./ RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps ``` Keep the builder stage at `NODE_ENV=production` (devDeps already installed by deps stage). ### Cloudflare SSL Mode for New Subdomains New proxied (orange cloud) subdomains default to **Full (Strict)** mode in Cloudflare. Pattern: same as productionizing any MVP site — add pages + Docker hardening + SEO + content localization. ## Common Pitfalls: ### Deep Business Research Before building any client site, run the full research pipeline — see `references/deep-business-research.md`. This includes: scraping competitor sites for all images, mapping GPS coordinates, identifying related companies/subsidiaries, finding director/owner names via press interviews, and documenting cross-sell opportunities. ### Promo carousels: badge + title + description - Blog category filtering: accented chars need NFD normalization before comparison - Dynamic page titles: reset on unmount to prevent stale titles - Social proof popups: position above WhatsApp float (bottom-32) - Age gates: use timestamp-based expiry, not boolean localStorage flags - Stats: initialize with "---" not "0" to avoid flash before animation - Accessibility: add skip-to-content link with #main-content target - Filter pagination: reset page to 1 when any filter changes - Product images: SVG files in /public/images/ exist but es.json must reference them by correct path. "✦" placeholder means the image path is wrong or missing. - Footer social dedup: if social bar (icon links) AND footer column (text links) both point to same Insta/WA, remove the text link column. - Title template in layout.tsx shows default even with dynamic page titles -- client components set document.title in useEffect, but SSR output still shows the default until hydration. - Price range sliders: two stacked range inputs overlap visually when values are close. Use a single range input (max price only) for simplicity. - Anon review forms: must have `formRef.current?.reset()` + setRating state on submit. Always strip combining diacritics when generating URL slugs. - Guest mode banner: use localStorage with 24h timestamp expiry, not sessionStorage (which shows on every new tab) or simple boolean (which never shows again). - Page metadata/layout.tsx: the `title` export in metadata is SSR-only. Badge needs `mr-1.5`, description needs `ml-2`, title needs proper whitespace. 2. **Blog content**: is the full article content rendering, or just the excerpt? If content lives in es.json, check it's long enough. Must use proper JS interpolation. 4. **Stats animation**: initial state is "0" or "—"? "0" causes flash of broken numbers. Use "—". 5. **Age gate**: boolean flag or timestamp? Timestamp with 7-day expiry is more resilient. 6. **Social proof popup**: does it overlap the WhatsApp float? Position at `bottom-32` or lower. 7. **Footer**: social platform links appear in both a column AND an icon bar? Remove the text-link column. 8. **Blog category URLs**: accented chars (Guías → guias)? Must NFD-normalize before filter comparison. 9. **Contact page**: has hours, address, multiple topic-specific buttons? Not just a single WhatsApp link. 10. **Privacy/Terms pages**: 8+ substantive sections, not 3-4 one-liners. 11. **Product images**: "✦" anywhere on product cards = image path broken or missing. SVGs should render. 12. **Page titles**: browser tab shows product name, not just site default. Check with `document.title` after hydration. 13. **Breadcrumbs**: visible on product/detail pages, not just hidden JSON-LD in ``. 14. **Accessibility**: skip-to-content link present? `#main-content` target exists? Dark mode toggle has correct `aria-label`? 15. **Duplicate badges**: "Principiante" showing in both LevelBadge and SafetyBadges? Only one should show it. 16. **Price slider**: dual range inputs overlap visually? Replace with single max-price slider. 17. **Anon review form**: reset on submit? Stars are clickable? Form resets with `formRef.current?.reset()`. 18. **Guest mode banner**: shows every new tab (sessionStorage)? Use localStorage with 24h timestamp instead. When the client is a band, artist, or creative brand (no products, no services catalog), the e-commerce template is wrong. Build custom pages.

### Page Structure for Band Sites

- **Home**: Hero headline/subheadline, bio section, feature cards (discography, shows, members), Spotify embed iframe, discography cards with album art, team grid (members + role + bio), testimonials, newsletter signup, booking CTA
- **Música**: Full discography with album art per item (from Spotify CDN), track listing per album, Spotify embed
- **Videos**: YouTube embed cards — extract video ID from URL with regex `/watch\?v=([^&]+)/`
- **Letras**: Song title + album metadata, lyrics displayed as lines in a styled dark box
- **Shows**: Event cards with date, venue, description — Calendar icon per card
- **Galería**: Photo grid with category filter buttons (Todas / En Vivo / Banda / Festivales), hover overlay on images
- **Contacto**: Contact cards for each channel (WhatsApp, Email, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, Spotify) — each with icon + label + hover color matching the platform brand

### Content Structure (es.json)

```json
{
  "siteName": "Nüdo",
  "tagline": "Hardcore Metal desde Capiatá, Paraguay",
  "contact": { "email", "instagram", "facebook", "spotify", "youtube" },
  "navigation": { "items": [{ "label", "href" }], "ctaText": "Escuchanos en Spotify", "ctaHref": "..." },
  "home": { "hero": { "headline", "subheadline", "ctaPrimaryText/Href", "ctaSecondaryText/Href", "trustBadges" }, "bio", "features" },
  "team": [{ "name", "role", "bio" }],
  "discography": [{ "title", "year", "type", "label", "imageUrl" (spotify CDN), "spotifyUrl", "tracks": [] }],
  "lyrics": [{ "title", "album", "lyrics": [line, line, ...] }],
  "shows": [{ "title", "date", "venue", "description" }],
  "gallery": [{ "src", "alt", "category" }],
  "videos": [{ "title", "url", "thumbnail" }],
  "testimonials": [{ "quote", "author" }],
  "newsletter": { "title", "subtitle", "placeholder", "submitLabel" },
  "footer": { "copyright" },
  "seo": { "defaultTitle", "defaultDescription", "ogImage" }
}
```

### Visual Identity for Band Sites

- **Dark theme**: `#0d0d0d` background, `#1a1a1a` sections, `#222` cards, `#333` borders
- **Primary accent**: `#8B0000` (dark red) for CTAs and highlights, hover `#B22222`
- **Text**: `#f5f5f5` foreground, `#a0a0a0` muted, `#666` metadata
- **Typography**: uppercase tracking-wider on metadata, all-caps navigation, impactful headings
- **Section gradients**: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0d0d0d 0%, #1a0000 50%, #0d0d0d 100%)` for hero sections
- **Spotify embed**: 352px height iframe, centered section with title + caption

### Docker: No public/ dir Edge Case

Band sites often have no `public/` directory. Remove the `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` line from Dockerfile to avoid build failure ("/app/public: not found").

### Deployment

Same as standalone store: build → deploy via Docker Swarm + Traefik. DNS A record to VPS IP, Cloudflare proxied.

{"action": "write_file", "file_path": "references/band-site-pattern.md", "name": "standalone-ecommerce-store", "content": "# Band / Creative Client Site Pattern

## When to Use

The e-commerce template is wrong. The client has no products to sell, no service catalog. They need a brand/presence site — band, artist, musician, photographer, creative portfolio.

## Content Source

All text comes from `content/es.json`. No database. No API. Fully static.

The content is typically extracted from a monorepo (e.g., paragu-ai-builder sites/nudo/) and converted into flat JSON.

## Conversion from Monorepo to Standalone

The monorepo stores sites as:

```
sites/<name>/
  site.json         — config (domain, contact, social, features)
  content/es.json   — all page text in builder schema
  content/band-bio.md — raw bios
  pages/*.json     — page configs (for builder renderer)
  images/          — assets (brand, hero, gallery)
  QUESTIONNAIRE.md — client questions
  ASSETS_INVENTORY.md — asset tracking
```

When extracting to standalone:

1. Flatten `site.json` contact/social fields into top-level `es.json` keys
2. Map builder's `content/es.json` keys to the standalone schema (home.hero, home.bio, team, discography, shows, gallery, videos, lyrics)
3. Discard `pages/*.json` (page routing is now filesystem-based in Next.js)
4. Discard builder-specific chrome config (header/footer config — now in components)
5. Images stay in the builder repo — reference Spotify CDN URLs for album art, Unsplash for placeholder gallery

## Common Pitfalls

- **No `public/` dir**: Band sites often have no public assets → the Dockerfile `COPY ./public` line crashes the build. Remove that line.
- **Static prerender**: All pages are `○ (Static)` in build output. If any page uses `useClient` with dynamic data, add `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"`.
- **Spotify embed source**: Use `https://open.spotify.com/embed/artist/{id}` with `utm_source=generator`. Width 100%, height 352px.
- **YouTube video ID extraction**: Regex `/(?:youtube\.com\/watch\?v=|youtu\.be\/)([^&]+)/` from the video URL.
- **WhatsApp number**: Store with country code prefix, strip non-digits with `/\D/g` before encoding in wa.me link.

## Verification

1. `npm run build` — all pages compile as static
2. `curl -s http://localhost:3000/` | grep Nüdo — server responds
3. All 7 pages render, Spotify embed loads, YouTube iframe loads
"}
